calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jun-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court stated that this is the co-trustee's petition to approve the accounting, that objections have been withdrawn, that the competing petition has been withdrawn, that the trust is not under court supervision but that the court expected the parties to be present today. The Court granted the accounting petition (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Apr-12-2016 Continued To Apr-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Continued To Apr-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Pet. To Compel Account, To Remove Cotrustee Kathleen Hicks For Hostility Toward & Lack Of Cooperation With Cotrustee;To Immediately Suspend Powers Etc
Off Calendar Mar-18-2016 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Continued To Apr-08-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Minger for (petitioner) Sandra Jackson. Charles Triay (via court call) for (co-trustees) Kathleen Hicks and Henry Jackson.

The Court stated that at the last hearing there was one issue remaining and asked for status. Ms. Minger stated that they are not done and that they are here to set the matter for trial. Mr. Triay reported that there was an issue finding money for Ms. Jackson to move from the home, that the money has been found but she is now having a hard time finding a place, that the trial is in regards to his clients accounting, that a lot of the issues have been resolved and that he thinks it will be a 1/2 day trial. The Court confirmed that the issues covered by the settlement were still resolved and would not be brought to trial. Mr. Triay stated that there are two separate issues, the accounting and objections and getting Ms. Jackson to move and that there had been many accounting issues but they have been narrowed down. Ms. Minger stated that there would possibly be 2-3 witnesses but that she thinks it can go on the papers and Mr. Triay agreed. The Court confirmed that the only issues are a couple of accounting issues, set the trial for April 8, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court requested that a joint statement of issues be filed by February 23, 2016, that the parties respective witness and exhibit lists be filed by March 11, 2016 and that trial briefs must be in by March 30, 2016. The accounting petition and the petition to compel account, etc., were continued to April 8, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Pet. To Compel Account, To Remove Cotrustee Kathleen Hicks For Hostility Toward & Lack Of Cooperation With Cotrustee;To Immediately Suspend Powers Etc
Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Continued To Apr-08-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Dec-08-2015 Continued To Jan-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Minger for beneficiary Sandra Jackson; Charles Triay (by CourtCall) for co-trustees Kathleen Hicks and Henry Jackson.

Ms. Minger reported that the parties are very close to resolving their dispute, but that one sticking point remained. Mr. Triay stated that the problem was money, as Ms. Jackson needs money to be able to move out of the house so it can be repaired and sold, but the trustees had distributed all the liquid assets before counsel were involved, and there is no money remaining in the trust to cover moving costs or the repair costs to get the property ready for marketing. The Court stated that this was something the parties should be able to work out, and the matter was continued to January 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for a further status hearing on settlement or moving forward with litigation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)

Pet. To Compel Account, To Remove Cotrustee Kathleen Hicks For Hostility Toward & Lack Of Cooperation With Cotrustee;To Immediately Suspend Powers Etc
Off Calendar Dec-08-2015 Continued To Jan-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Nov-10-2015 Continued To Dec-01-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Minger for Sandra Jackson. Charles Triay (via court call) for Kathleen Hicks and Henry Jackson.

The Court asked for a status on the mediation. Ms. Minger reported that the mediation did not succeed but that progress has been made toward settlement and that the parties are talking to each other. Mr. Triay stated his agreement and requested that the matter be continued to see if they can settle. The Accounting petition and the Petition to compel account, to remove co-trustee Kathleen Hicks for Hostility, etc., were continued to December 1, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Pet. To Compel Account, To Remove Cotrustee Kathleen Hicks For Hostility Toward & Lack Of Cooperation With Cotrustee;To Immediately Suspend Powers Etc
Off Calendar Nov-10-2015 Continued To Dec-01-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Sep-15-2015 Continued To Nov-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Triay for (co-trustees) Kathleen Hicks & Henry Jackson. Marilyn Minger (via court call) for Sandra Jackson.

Mr. Triay reported that they went to mediation, that there has not been a resolution, that they have not set another mediation date and that there will be additional settlement discussions. Ms. Minger stated her agreement and that she believes there is a possibility of resolution. The accounting petition and the petition to compel and accounting, etc., were continued to November 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel -- Civil
Off Calendar Jun-30-2015 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2015 Continued To Aug-25-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn E. Minger for and with Co-Trustee Sandra Jackson; Charles Triay appearing by CourtCall for Kathleen Hicks and Henry Jenkins.

The Court noted that there were three matters on calendar. Ms. Minger stated that one of these was her motiion to be relieved as counsel, and she requested that that matter go off calendar, as she had re-established communications with her client. She stated that she had been in discussion with Mr. Triay, and that the parties agreed to go to mediation of the other two matters. The Court ordered the motion to be relieved as counsel off calendar, and inquired whether the parties desired to participate in the Court's pro bono mediation program, as the matter met the qualifications for that program. Ms. Minger stated that the parties had not gotten that far in their discussions, but that she believed proceeding by pro bono mediation was appropriate. Mr. Triay also agreed to proceed through the Court's pro bono mediation program.
The Court set a mediation status hearing date of August 25, 2015 at 9:00 am in Department 204. He signed the order referring the case to pro bono mediation, requiring the mediation be completed by August 17, 2015, and notification to the Probate Examiner of the status of the mediation by August 19, 2015. The hearing on the remaining two petitions was continued to August 25, 2015 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Nov-19-2014 Continued To Jan-05-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marilyn Minger for and with Sandra Jackson (Petitioner). Charles Triay for Kathleen Hicks (Respondent).

Ms. Minger stated that they would like an accounting and that they would like to have it filed within the first two weeks in December. Mr. Triay stated that they have agreed to prepare an accounting and to a starting date, that he received a draft of the accounting and that he can have it filed by the end of the year. The Petition to Compel Account, etc. was continued to January 5, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
